stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, proveAux (list_degree Γ + tp_degree A) Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)

Introduce a proof predicate for singleton sequents that permits only ax, ldiv_r, and rdiv_r, and prove: for all A C : Tp, [A] ⇒ C if and only if there exists such a left-rule-free derivation of [A] ⇒ C.

Introduce an inductive proof predicate for sequents from atom-only contexts using only `ax`, `ldiv_r`, and `rdiv_r`, and prove: for all `{Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}`, `(∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) → ((Γ ⇒ A) ↔ AtomOnlyNormalDerivation Γ A)`.

Define what it means for a type and a context to lie entirely in the left-only fragment or entirely in the right-only fragment, and prove: if every formula in `Γ` lies in the left-only fragment, then `(Γ ⇒ # s)` in the full product-free calculus iff the corresponding translated context derives `# s` in the left-only calculus; likewise for the right-only fragment.

Prove a mixed-handed boundary theorem for the recursive support invariant: if every formula in Γ lies entirely in the left-only fragment or entirely in the right-only fragment, then for every atom s, support_ok Γ (# s) is equivalent to the naive support condition ∃ B ∈ Γ, occurs_atom s B; moreover, any failure of this flat condition in the full calculus requires genuinely mixed-handed context formulas.

Define what it means for an arbitrary type to lie entirely in the left-only fragment or entirely in the right-only fragment, and prove: if every formula in Γ and A is left-only, then Γ ⇒ A in the full product-free calculus iff the corresponding translated sequent is derivable in the left-only calculus; dually, if every formula in Γ and A is right-only, then full derivability is equivalent to derivability in the right-only calculus.

Define the collection of formulas occurring anywhere in a candidate tree, and prove a global subformula property: whenever Γ ⇒ A, there exists a candidate tree for Γ and A in which every intermediate formula is a subformula of some formula in Γ or of A.

Construct the finite state graph generated by all subformula-bounded candidate-tree decompositions of a fixed sequent, and prove that Γ ⇒ A holds exactly when the root state (Γ, A) is accepted by that graph.

Prove that if every formula in Γ and A lies entirely in the left-only fragment, then every global proof-search decomposition of Γ ⇒ A stays entirely in the left-only fragment; dually, if every formula lies in the right-only fragment, every such decomposition stays entirely in the right-only fragment.

Prove a cross-fragment rigidity theorem: if every formula in Γ is left-only and A is right-only, then Γ ⇒ A implies A is atomic; dually, if every formula in Γ is right-only and A is left-only, then Γ ⇒ A implies A is atomic.

Define intrinsic candidate-tree notions for the left-only and right-only calculi, and prove that on one-sided sequents the translation to the full calculus induces a size-preserving bijection between fragment candidate trees and full candidate trees.

Define a subformula relation on `Tp`, and prove that whenever `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)` and `AtomicCandidateTree Δ s`, every formula appearing anywhere in that atomic search tree lies in the subformula closure of the original sequent `(Γ, A)`.
